page_header.subtitle: Our Story
page_header.title: About the Players
page_header.description: Over 70 years of bringing drama to the heart of a small Oxfordshire village.

intro.heading: A Village Tradition Since 1953
paragraphs: The Stonesfield Players were formed in the village of Stonesfield, near Witney, in 1953. For over seven decades we've been entertaining audiences in West Oxfordshire with productions ranging from Shakespearean classics to original one-act plays, from beloved pantomimes to modern drama.

Most years there has been at least one production, and they have ranged from farce to Shakespeare, from panto to drama. In more recent years, several of our productions have been written especially for us by members of the Players.
We perform at Stonesfield Village Hall — a warm, intimate venue that brings audiences right into the heart of the action.

notable_members.name: Rupert Friend
notable_members.description: Best known for Homeland and Hitman: Agent 47, Rupert was a member of the Players throughout his teenage years before going on to a stellar film and TV career.

notable_members.name: Sally Wainwright
notable_members.description: The acclaimed writer and director has been a member of the Players, directing several productions over the years. We even premiered one of her one-act plays in the 1990s.

notable_members.name: Becki Sherlaw-Johnson
notable_members.description: Our longest-serving member, Becki joined as a teenager in 1975 and has been a driving force behind the group — acting, directing and serving as secretary.

timeline.title: The Beginning
timeline.description: The Stonesfield Players are founded in the village of Stonesfield, near Witney, Oxfordshire.

timeline.title: Growing Membership
timeline.description: Becki Sherlaw-Johnson joins as a teenager — she would go on to become the group's longest-serving member.

timeline.title: Original Works
timeline.description: The Players premiere a one-act play by Sally Wainwright and begin producing more original work written by members.

timeline.title: Competition Success
timeline.description: The group enters local drama competitions and wins both junior and senior categories. Young member Rupert Friend begins his acting journey.

timeline.title: Cold Comfort Farm
timeline.description: A brilliant production of Stella Gibbons' classic at Stonesfield Village Hall, directed by Becki Sherlaw-Johnson. Three nights, sold-out audiences.

timeline.title: Mercy of Sisters
timeline.description: Our next production — coming to the Village Hall in late June 2026. Watch this space!

productions.title: Cold Comfort Farm
productions.description: Stella Gibbons' brilliantly funny portrayal of English rural life in the 1930s, dramatised by Paul Doust and directed by Becki Sherlaw-Johnson.

productions.title: The Private Collection
productions.description: An original play written by Becki Sherlaw-Johnson and David Lines. Featuring Katie Saville and Ruth Thornburn.

productions.title: Plaza Suite
productions.description: Neil Simon's classic comedy, featuring Beth Binnian, Mark Saville and Alison Richards.

productions.title: Twelfth Night
productions.description: Shakespeare's beloved comedy — one of many classic works the Players have performed over the decades.

productions.title: Mother Goose
productions.description: One of several pantomimes the Players have produced, delighting audiences young and old.

productions.title: Original One-Act Play
productions.description: A world premiere of a one-act play by Sally Wainwright, performed by the Players.

cta.heading: Want to Join Us?
cta.description: Whether you want to tread the boards, work backstage, or just help out on show nights — we'd love to hear from you.
